title: Lifecycle, Storage Classes, Archive Restore, and Cost · Execution Trace: Lifecycle Rule Precedence
summary: A policy transitions active content immediately, expires current versions but not hidden history, and promises instant archive access without retrieval or minimum-duration cost. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the lifecycle rule precedence increment in lifecycle and storage-cost decision table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evi…
objectives: Model filters, current and noncurrent transitions, expiration, delete markers, multipart cleanup, precedence, propagation, and unsupported bucket features.
Select storage class from access frequency, object size, retrieval latency, resilience, minimum duration, monitoring, and transition cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Lifecycle Rule Precedence · Theory Lifecycle Rule Precedence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 6225 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to evaluate lifecycle transition, retention lock, restore, and minimum-duration cost; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Lifecycle Rule Precedence contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Lifecycle Rule Precedence · Theory Lifecycle Rule Precedence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model filters, current and noncurrent transitions, expiration, delete markers, multipart cleanup, precedence, propagation, and unsupported bucket features.
